description: BEGINNER EXAMPLE - a MODULAR (heterogeneous) TDCR. Instead of one uniform material, the robot is assembled from reusable 'modules' defined in module_library; each module has its own material, geometry and color. Here a stiff base grades into a soft, flexible tip. Module/segment boundaries are rigid joins. Generate it with:  python generate.py --config example_modular --franka
_comment_modular: modular: true switches on the module system. With it, you list 'module_library' + per-segment 'modules' instead of the single material_properties used by the standard example.
_comment_library: =================== MODULE LIBRARY ===================  Reusable building blocks. Each module: length (m), radius (visual, m), num_links, color (RGBA), and its own material_properties (youngs_modulus in Pa sets stiffness; damping_ratio scales damping with stiffness; outer_radius sets the structural cross-section).
_comment_segments: =================== SEGMENTS ===================  Each segment is a list of modules (built tip-ward) plus its tendon actuation. Reusing modules makes the gradient obvious: stiff base -> mixed middle -> soft tip.

segments._comment: Segment 1 (base): two stiff modules - resists bending.
Segment 3 (tip): two soft modules - bends easily.

_comment_joints: joints_per_link: 2 = bending about two axes. Ranges are per-joint limits in degrees.
_comment_actuation: Tendon actuation (same options as the standard example).
_comment_collision: enable_tdcr_collision: let the backbone collide with task objects; link_shape: link geometry.
